comment_text:
Bear with me my new hyper-fixation;
Movies are a form of storytelling.
Storytelling has been humanities vehicle since the first word, communication has evolved us as a race collectively.
Thats why cinema is very valubale to me. They remind me of my humanity and identity.
This movie is the most perfect movie i have ever seen. Its a masterpiece than any other i have ever seen.
It is the deepest. The story is simple. But the messages, metaphores, symbolisms are the deepest in human philosophy.
The meaning of life, the father and son/ god and son comparison, the generational abuse, the lesson of ego and playing god, the selfishness vs selflesness, the lesson of finding meaning when there is no meaning, the value of life and burden of eternity, finding identity, the nihilism. Life is meaningful when it is limitted.
And with all that it is  assembled with symboles and metaphores, perfect writing, perfect storytelling, perfect pace, equal dosage of chaos and peace, equal dosage of action and relaxation, artwork cinematography, perfect casting, perfect acting, expert music score, and closure. I missed tragedies, i missed cinema with effort.
Masterpiece. Perfect.

comment_text:
Frankenstein (2025) - one of the most HUMAN films I’ve ever seen!!
A story where you feel how the wounds we inherit from our parents keep living inside us, shaping our choices, our fears, even the way we love. And how easily we pass these wounds forward, often without noticing. Yet there is always a point where choice and free will becomes possible. You can always end the circle of pain.
 The film shows that ambition often grows from fear. The wish of Viktor to overcome death and to prove his worth to the world. But Creature turned out to be more human then his creator, with baby innocence in the world. He is the one who teaches Viktor forgiveness. 
Guillermo del Toro created Frankenstein like a handmade masterpiece, with minimal digital effects and with so much details in colors, shadows, costumes, textured .. And it becomes clear in the end, that a monster is not the one who is born different, but the one who turns away from love.

video_title: Frankenstein | Guillermo del Toro | Official Teaser | Netflix
comment_text: I know I’ll probably be a lone voice in my opinion but I left the cinema feeling empty, not because of my emotive response to the monster, but because I wasn’t affected emotionally by the story and yet, I arrived with a complete willingness to be touched by the film. Robert de Niro’s portrayal of the creature in the 1994 is heart wrenching just as the final chapter in the book when the creature pleads his case. At this point, you are completely consumed by the beauty and the malevolence of humanity. I agree the film is a visual feast with masterful imagery, design, sound, costume and story. For me though, the clean lines of the prosthetics failed to evoke the grotesque of the monster which surely, is the heart of human prejudice! The story has additional characters who are not part of the original story. I try not to compare books with films as they are often separate entities in their own rights. The film tips into more fantasy and science fiction that is fitting with the Director but less so for my literary sensibilities.

comment_text:
My personal opinion about this film is generally positive 👍
It is not some epic masterpiece that one will remember for a lifetime and in my opinion  Oscar Isaac as Victor Frankenstein is not the best casting, but the film literally surprised me positively 👏
First of all: finally - NO gay and black propaganda !!!
For which applause again to the director 👏
It also lacks that crazy Hollywood feminism.
Frankenstein's creature in my opinion is shown quite well as a blank sheet of paper on which whatever you write, that is what you will read.
Real film music performed by a symphony orchestra instead of those typical sound experiments in most films like for example Tron Ares.
Was the film a little longer than it should be ?
For me personally – yes.
Maybe 15 minutes shorter would have been perfect in duration, but Del Toro likes long stories.
He still could have asked some Germans in Hollywood what the name ,,Frankenstein" really means = the stone of the Franks !
Also, it seems Guillermo is not aware, that if you can hold a stick of dynamite in your hand without the explosion tearing your body apart, then most likely most bullets will not be able to penetrate that same body.
But I stop with the criticism because my opinion is positive and overall I personally recommend this film specifically to all who are fans of the story.

comment_text: “Frankenstein” has been a passion project of director Guillermo del Toro’s for a long time, but you don’t need to read about how he worked for more than a decade to get it off the ground to understand that. The book may be over a century old, but it's still a better yarn than any of the screen adaptations I've seen to date.

comment_text: The feedback on this from Venice has been largely positive. Guillermo del Toro's detractors think it's style over substance, but others have praised it's faithfulness to Mary Shelley's novel, the production design, lack of major CGI, Alexandre Desplat's music score, the emotional levels as well as gore/scares & the cast delivering superb performances - especially Jacob Elordi's Creature who has Best Supporting Actor awards buzz. I hope the 3 week cinema window hits near my neck of the woods as this is a big screen event for sure. As a fan of the book, previous movie versions & director Guillermo del Toro, I can't wait! 😃
